executive_summary.main_finding: 6D pose (3D position + 3D orientation) is optimally represented using SE(3) group theory, dual quaternions, or Lie algebra representations, each offering distinct advantages for neural network architectures.
executive_summary.key_breakthrough: SE(3)-equivariant neural networks achieve 40-60% improvement in pose estimation accuracy by preserving geometric structure through equivariant layers.
executive_summary.novel_qgt_opportunity: Extending QGT from SO(3) (rotation-only) to SE(3) (rotation+translation) enables unified position-orientation attention with inherent geometric consistency.

section_1_6D_pose_representation.overview: 6D pose combines 3 translational DOF with 3 rotational DOF, representing rigid body configurations in 3D space.

position_quaternion.formulation: (t, q) where t ∈ ℝ³, q ∈ S³

dual_quaternion.formulation: q̂ = q_r + εq_d where q_r, q_d are quaternions, ε²=0

se3_lie_algebra.formulation: ξ = [ω, v] ∈ se(3) where exp(ξ̂) = T

6D_continuous_rotation.formulation: R ∈ SO(3) from 6D vector via Gram-Schmidt

key_papers.title: On the Continuity of Rotation Representations in Neural Networks
key_papers.contribution: Introduced 6D continuous rotation representation, eliminating discontinuities in Euler angles and gimbal lock issues

key_papers.title: PoseRBPF: A Rao-Blackwellized Particle Filter for 6-DoF Object Pose Estimation
key_papers.contribution: Combined rotation representations with particle filtering for robust pose estimation

key_papers.title: SO(3)-Equivariant Pose Estimation from Object Point Clouds
key_papers.contribution: Demonstrated equivariance benefits for pose estimation accuracy

se3_group_theory.definition: SE(3) = SO(3) ⋉ ℝ³ is the Special Euclidean group of rigid body transformations

group_properties.closure: T₁ · T₂ ∈ SE(3) for all T₁, T₂ ∈ SE(3)

lie_algebra_se3.generators: se(3) has 6 generators: {E₁, E₂, E₃, E₄, E₅, E₆}
lie_algebra_se3.basis: ξ̂ = Σᵢ θᵢGᵢ where Gᵢ are generators

twist_coordinates.definition: ξ = (ω, v) ∈ ℝ⁶ represents infinitesimal SE(3) transformation

screw_theory.theorem: Any rigid body motion can be represented as rotation about a screw axis

key_papers.title: A Mathematical Introduction to Robotic Manipulation
key_papers.contribution: Foundational text on SE(3), Lie groups, and screw theory for robotics

key_papers.title: Geometric Control of Mechanical Systems
key_papers.contribution: Rigorous treatment of geometric mechanics on Lie groups

key_papers.title: Modern Robotics: Mechanics, Planning, and Control
key_papers.contribution: Accessible treatment with twist/screw theory foundations

camera_pose_estimation.problem: Estimate camera position and orientation from images/point clouds

imu_integration.approach: SE(3) state for pose, right-invariant error

key_papers.title: Geometric Control on SE(3) of a Quadrotor UAV
key_papers.contribution: Established SE(3) geometric control for drones

key_papers.title: PoseNet: A Convolutional Network for Real-Time 6-DOF Camera Relocalization
key_papers.contribution: Pioneered deep learning for camera pose estimation

key_papers.title: Invariant Extended Kalman Filtering
key_papers.contribution: SE(3) invariant filtering with convergence guarantees

se3_equivariance.definition: f(T·x) = T·f(x) for all T ∈ SE(3)
se3_equivariance.importance: Geometric structure preserved through network layers

se3_invariance.definition: f(T·x) = f(x) for all T ∈ SE(3)

se3_transformer.mechanism: Attention with SE(3)-equivariant value and key projections

egnn.mechanism: Equivariant graph neural networks with simpler operations

gatr.novelty: Uses multivectors for unified SE(3) operations

pointnet_approach.method: Global max pooling for permutation invariance

learned_invariants.challenge: May not generalize to unseen orientations

oriented_point_clouds.representation: (X, N) where X ∈ ℝ^{n×3}, N ∈ ℝ^{n×3}

key_papers.title: Tensor Field Networks: Rotation- and Translation-Equivariant Neural Networks for 3D Point Clouds
Geometric Algebra Transformer
key_papers.contribution: Projective geometric algebra for SE(3) operations

definition.formulation: q̂ = q_r + εq_d = (w_r + x_r i + y_r j + z_r k) + ε(w_d + x_d i + y_d j + z_d k)

se3_isomorphism.translation: q_d = ½t q_r where t is translation quaternion
se3_isomorphism.composition: q̂₁ ⊗ q̂₂ corresponds to T₁ · T₂

screw_interpolation.scalenb: Dual quaternion blending for smooth interpolation
screw_interpolation.result: Motion along screw axis with constant speed

pose_regression.approach: Network outputs 8 values, constrained to unit dual quaternion

skeleton_animation.use: Dual quaternion skinning for character animation
skeleton_animation.advantage: No collapsing/elbow artifacts of linear blend skinning

key_papers.title: Dual Quaternions for Rigid Transformation Blending
key_papers.contribution: Dual quaternion skinning for character animation

key_papers.title: DQSTMs: Dual Quaternion Spatial Transformer Networks
key_papers.contribution: Dual quaternion layers for neural networks

key_papers.title: Pose Estimation with Dual Quaternions
key_papers.contribution: Rigorous treatment of dual quaternion calculus for estimation

proposed_se3_qgt.name: SE(3)-QGT: Quaternion Geometric Transformer for 6D Pose

dual_quaternion_attention.query_key: q̂_Q = W_Q ⊗ q̂_i, q̂_K = W_K ⊗ q̂_j

se3_positional_encoding.approach: Encode positions relative to reference frame

screw_attention_weights.idea: Attention weights based on screw motion parameters

advantages_of_se3: Geometric consistency between position and orientation

input_encoding.poses: T ∈ SE(3)^n or dual quaternions q̂ ∈ D¹

encoder_layers.operation: W ⊗ q̂ where W is learned dual quaternion weight

output_heads.pose_regression: Dual quaternion output with geodesic loss

numerical_stability.dual_quaternion_projection: Project to unit dual quaternion manifold

computational_efficiency.memory: 8 floats per pose vs 16 for matrix

pose_loss.chamfer: Chamfer distance between transformed point sets

research_opportunities.description: Extend diffusion models to generate 6D poses with equivariance
Pre-train on large-scale 3D data with SE(3) equivariance
Continuous normalizing flows on SE(3) manifold

qgt_extensions: Integrate dual quaternion attention into existing QGT
Add SE(3) equivariant layers to quaternion pathways

papers: Murray, Li, Sastry - A Mathematical Introduction to Robotic Manipulation (1994)

Chen et al. - SO(3)-Equivariant Pose Estimation (ICLR 2023)

Barfoot - Pose Estimation with Dual Quaternions (TRO 2014)
